description: Production Readiness: Synthesis verdict — aggregate multiple high-severity risk signals.

fixtures.notes: SQL query built via string interpolation (injection), user-supplied input passed directly to the database, and no error handling — three compounding high-severity signals with no tests changed.

Blocking HTTP call in async init() stalls the event loop; no timeout on the request; no error handling around the JSON parse; and zero test coverage of the change — four high-severity signals stacked together.

Removes per-battle locking (race condition reintroduced), adds a shared mutable dict pop() call outside the lock, and removes battle_tag cleanup in the finish handler — three interacting state-corruption vectors with no test changes.

Converts async polling function to sync (blocking event loop), uses broad except to swallow all errors including non-transient failures, and removes asyncio.to_thread safety wrapper — three high-severity signals: blocking I/O, silent error swallowing, and behavioral regression.

Removes IDisposable implementation (resource leak on every page render), introduces Task.Run().Result blocking in UI property getter, and drops the null-check guard — memory leak plus deadlock risk with no tests.
Migrates SQL string interpolation to parameterized queries, adds multi-statement injection detection, and wraps execution in try/catch — clean security hardening with no new risks introduced.
Listener registration now guards startup sequencing to avoid deadlocks without introducing additional high-severity production risk signals.
